channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Experts warn of AI chatbots offering mental health advice
item.description: The American Psychological Association is warning about the growing use of chatbots "masquerading" as licensed mental health professionals. They cite two situations as examples.
item.dc:creator: David Lazarus, KTLA 5

item.title: Hegseth says he fired the top military lawyers because they weren't well suited for the jobs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Monday that he was replacing the top lawyers for the military services because he didn't think they were “well-suited” to provide recommendations when lawful orders are given.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump backs Musk as he roils the federal workforce with demands and threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump backed Elon Musk's demand that federal employees explain their recent accomplishments by the end of Monday or risk getting fired, even as government agency officials were told that compliance with Musk's edict was voluntary.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: West Springs Hospital announces closure
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — In a news release Monday, West Springs Hospital announced that after 20 years of operation here on the Western Slope, they will be closing their doors. "I don't know much about anything.
item.dc:creator: Alex Rivero, KREX 5

item.title: Mississippi city drops lawsuit over newspaper editorial that judge ordered removed
item.description: A Mississippi city dropped its lawsuit Monday against a newspaper that had its editorial criticizing local leaders removed by a judge in a case that sparked widespread outrage from First Amendment advocates.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bill filed in Arkansas legislature to monitor fertility, embryo reproduction and outcomes
item.description: Included in the requirements is for fertility clinics to track how many embryos are created, how many are destroyed and how and why the decision was made.
item.dc:creator: Alex Kienlen, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Canada and Mexico tariffs are 'going forward' with more import taxes to come
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Monday that his tariffs on Canada and Mexico are starting next month, ending a monthlong suspension on the planned import taxes that could potentially hurt economic growth and worsen inflation.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and FABIOLA SANCHEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court declines to take up challenge to Tennessee law restricting drag shows
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to hear a challenge to a Tennessee law restricting some drag performances, allowing the first-in-the-nation law to remain largely intact.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inmate death reported as illegal prison guard strike stretches into 2nd week
item.description: COXSACKIE, N.Y. (AP) — A wildcat strike by guards at New York's prisons, now in its second week, is fueling fears over deteriorating conditions behind bars.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, CEDAR ATTANASIO and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Supreme Court schedules arguments in case where Trump administration is defending ACA
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday scheduled arguments for April 21 in a case that could decide the legality of the Affordable Care Act’s (ACA) requirement that insurers cover certain preventive services.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Princeton man accused of killing former star athlete brother, cat with golf club, knife near Ivy League school
item.description: New Jersey authorities have arrested Princeton man Matthew Hertgen, who is accused of killing his brother, Joseph Hertgen, and a cat, allegedly with a knife and golf club.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell suggests Trump has ‘gross misunderstanding’ of Ukraine talks
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) on Monday suggested that President Trump’s refusal to blame Russia as the sole aggressor who provoked the war in Ukraine “reflects a gross misunderstanding of the nature of negotiations and leverage.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Catholic bishops sue Trump over refugee resettlement suspension
item.description: “We are urging the government to uphold its legal and moral obligations to refugees,” said the executive director of public affairs of the United States Conference of Catholic Bishops.
item.dc:creator: Susie Pinto, NewsNation

item.title: Ron DeSantis talks up his wife as next Florida governor and takes a shot at Trump's pick
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — As Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis approaches the end of his second term in office, he’s talking up one potential heir to succeed him in 2026: his wife, Casey DeSantis. And he's taking a shot at President Donald Trump 's pick to be the next governor.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge rejects immediately restoring AP's access to White House but urges government to reconsider
item.description: A federal judge has refused to immediately order the White House to restore The Associated Press’ access to presidential events, saying the news organization had not shown it had suffered irreparable harm in the matter.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sedensky, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Watch: Trump, Macron hold White House press conference
item.description: President Donald Trump hosts French President Emmanuel Macron on Monday, the first visit by a European leader to the White House since Trump returned.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KRON4

item.title: Police investigating report of hidden camera in RIC bathroom
item.description: A student employee told campus police he was using the bathroom on the second floor of Donovan Dining Center when he spotted what appeared to be a camera lens in the ceiling near a sprinkler head.
item.dc:creator: Tim White, NewsNation

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KTLA 5

item.title: Come 'Beyond the Gates' for the first Black-led soap opera
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The first thing viewers of “Beyond the Gates” see is a black Mercedes gliding past manicured lawns and stately estates. The sedan pulls up at an elegant country club and the valets come out. The Emotions' funky tune “Best of My Love” is playing.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Patients struggle with lack of consistent coverage for popular weight-loss drugs
item.description: Supplies of high-demand obesity treatments are improving, but that doesn’t mean they're easier to get.
item.dc:creator: Tom Murphy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Trump administration is firing 2,000 USAID workers and putting thousands of others on leave
item.description: The Trump administration said Sunday that it was placing all but a fraction of staffers at the U.S. Agency for International Development on leave worldwide and eliminating 2,000 U.S.-based staff positions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Avalanches in western US claim more lives in recent weeks including a weekend death in Colorado
item.description: WINTER PARK, Colo. (AP) — One person was killed in an avalanche in central Colorado on Saturday, marking just the latest fatality stemming from avalanches in the western U.S. this season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan migrants are flown home from Guantanamo Bay, with a layover in Honduras
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Nearly 200 Venezuelan immigrants to the U.S. were returned to their home country after being detained at Guantanamo Bay, in a flurry of flights that forged an unprecedented pathway for U.S. deportations. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Mikaela Shiffrin gets historic 100th World Cup race win and ties record for most podiums
item.description: Shiffrin’s record-extending 100th career World Cup race win Sunday fulfilled a quest put on hold by a serious crash in November.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lynne Marie Stewart of 'Pee-wee's Playhouse,' 'It's Always Sunny' dies: reports
item.description: The actress has recently finished filming for another season of "It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia" and her forthcoming pickleball-related film, "Dink.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Uber feature may overcharge you. Here's how to turn it off
item.description: Unless you disable it, a new setting in the app may end up costing you the next time you hail a ride overseas.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: USPS honors beloved actress Betty White with commemorative stamp
item.description: The stamp features a digitally created portrait of White against a violet background with "bubbly spots that befit her sparkling personality.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Pennsylvania Gov. Shapiro meets with family of fallen officer, victims of hospital shooting
item.description: "I am inspired by the amazing doctors, nurses, and staff at UPMC and WellSpan Health who did tremendous work to save lives in the face of tragedy," said Shapiro. "It was my honor to meet with them and to thank them for what they do in service to their neighbors.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KRON4

item.title: What to look for when buying a used car
item.description: With used car prices still at historic highs, it’s more important than ever before to be confident in your used car purchase.
item.dc:creator: ISeeCars, KRON4

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Fox 5
